title: 🚨 Midjourney V8 Launch Confirmed for Year-End! Super Draft, Style Finder, PCode System Revealed🔥

segments.text:
Hello.
This is Nam-kyoung Cho from Midjourney Korea. Today,

I'd like to share news from yesterday's Office Hour.
Before we get to the actual video, please subscribe , like, and

set notifications. They're a huge help in our YouTube production
. Please like, subscribe, and

set notifications.
The first update

is about the status of V8 development. The team's focus is on V8
, built on

the stable V7 codebase
. They say, "The core system has already been verified." This

is much faster than expected.
Regarding the V8 schedule, back in September,

we planned to do data verification in October and
then build the system over

the next two months, November and December.
It's now mid-October, and

we've already completed the data verification last week,
and the core system has been verified. Now,

we're scaling up the system and
training on a large-scale system with large data sets.

If there are no issues, V8 will be ready
. While running on a large

system takes a bit longer
than expected to prepare,

the actual execution
doesn't take that long. It requires a lot of computing power

, so it costs a bit, but
the execution itself

doesn't take that long. Reflecting this rapid progress,
we're aiming for a release before the end of the year.

Midjourney typically refers to
December 15th when they say "

the end of the year ," but
you can expect it to be around late November or early December

. While they do note that the schedule is subject to change, it's significantly
faster than the V8 release schedule we mentioned

in September
. It's roughly about a month ahead. V8

is expected to significantly improve
quality and consistency,

but that's the same expectation we have with every update
. They say "most of the engineering team is currently

focused on V8 development,
" but the developers

are splitting up.
Regarding V7.1 testing and features,

internal testing is underway
within the Guides and Moderators group

. This means "V7.1 is already out."
They've confirmed that

prompt interpretation accuracy
has improved, but they 've also said

"there are few major improvements," and "major improvements"
need to be visually noticeable

. The people in the guide and moderator
groups are incredibly

skilled at handling prompts
and are true Midjourney veterans. They

've clearly seen
a noticeable improvement in prompt interpretation

. However, for the average user
, the difference in prompt accuracy is difficult to perceive.

That's why
we've released a new feature

called "Big Improvements
." This

new feature is
a bit of a stretch, but it's still a small improvement . So,

we're testing out a personalization system, which we're currently testing
on a Black Ops server, a private experimental

server, where the next
version of the personalization system is currently being tested

. This means it could be a noticeable addition
to the Big Improvements

. Furthermore, we've implemented an enhanced
Draft mode, which focuses on sub-second

Super Draft mode creation . Both of these
improvements are being prepared

for V7.1, aiming for speed and quality
. If they don't decide

that it's 'clearly better',
they'll likely skip the public release.

Now that I think about it, V8 is scheduled to come out
around the end of November or early December,

so that leaves about two to a month and
a half. If that happens,

they'll release V7.1 in the meantime
, and bugs will arise, and if they fix these bug reports

and do this, the workforce
will be divided. Users, even

if they don't decide that it's 'clearly better', and
if they know that an announcement will require additional resources

anyway, they
'll think, 'If that's the case, I'll skip it

.' Internally at Midjourney,
we say that 7.1 'acts as a verification layer for the V8 system

.' Rather than focusing on improving the performance of
7.1 itself, we plan to use

it to test V8 features and such.
Even before V7 came out

, we had something similar to V6.1
and called it a 'verification layer' for V7

. However, V7
was delayed, so V6.1 ended

up being released. Considering that,
if V8 performs well, V7.1

might be skipped, but if V8's delay
gets worse, 7.1 might come out,

but for now,
I don't think they'll bother with 7.1.

There was news about Style Finder and Style Creator,
but Style Finder

disappeared last week and
came up again this time. Unlike V7.1 or V8,

this style-related thing
seems to be progressing well.

Style Finder
is said to be high-performance and "help

users quickly find the aesthetic style they want
," but its form is still unknown. However

, I think you can understand it
as "already completed ."

Some details about Style Creator
were revealed, and I was quite surprised

to see that it "creates
styles from scratch ." I had imagined

Style Creator
would use Style Seed Blends to mix Styles A and B

to create a new style, but since it's
talking about starting from scratch

, I think it's
possible to truly create styles

from scratch
. It's currently

being tested, but it's difficult to evaluate, so
I'm talking about the difficulty of evaluation

.
I created a style, but

is it really what I intended?
It's difficult to judge, and I think, "I like it," but I think, "It's different."

Will they think, 'People are also good.' This
is also a question mark, so

it's true that there's
a bit of an ambiguous way to objectively quantify

the generated styles .
They say that Style Finder is likely to be released soon

, but there are some question marks about Style Creator, and seeing that Style
Finder is said to have 'excellent performance,'

I think the original plan
was to release both together, but

since Creator was delayed, they decided to release Finder first
. Reflecting that thought, Style

Creator may be delayed as a next-generation feature
. They usually say things like this,

and then sometimes the clue
comes out and they continue to release.

Both Style Finder and Style Creator
are said to be 'closely connected to

the personalization and user profile system.'
This personalization refers to Pcode, but

this part seems to be creating something through ranking of personal preferred
styles

. And the user profile
system

can be thought of as 'Instagram's Midjourney version.'
Midjourney's Style

Finder, with
the features available in

Style Explorer, is a bit
like this. If you input

"Man" or "Manga" into the prompt,
you can use the keyword

search function in Fuzzy Search
to find two styles

like this . Of course, there are many more
. If you display the search styles like this

and ask , "Which style are you?"
and select this style,

you can search for similar styles
using this image.

You can also differentiate
between the line styles

within the Manga style and ask, "Do you want this line style
?" or " Do you want

more detail ?"
If you select this style,

you can also search for
styles using this image,

showcasing a more advanced
version of this style, with thin and rough

lines, like a shoujo manga
. If the user selects this

style , this is the final choice.
This isn't the exact UI, but it could be an

interface that shows the history of your
previous selections and says , "This is what you selected."

Ultimately, it
could display the Sref See of that style like this.

In the case of Style Finder, the foundation of the technology
is expected to be 4.2 billion SREF seeds . Besides this format,

you can also expect this type of display
when you input something like this

. What you're seeing now is
that I searched for "Manga Style"

in Style Explorer
and manually

created the images like this. Midjourney
can use the Midjourney API, so

it could be created
automatically . Photoshop

has a similar UI, right? If you center it and
select it , it can also show various options

on the side. I'm thinking
, "Wouldn't this style be possible?"

This news is related
to user profiles,

and it's actively being developed and
internally tested. It seems to be roughly

complete. They're going to release it soon with
Style Finder, and they said it's deeply connected

to user profiles. So, the styles
you find in Style Finder are shared

on your user profile
, and ultimately,

you can think of it as a Socal Media, Midjourney version of Instagram
. Just like sharing images on Instagram,

you share the styles you find in Style Finder, and they
also provide SREF seed information that follows

. When other users see this, they'll likely
follow you or like it, or something along those lines

. They say their goal is to improve
community discovery and personalization,

but while the specifics of
personalization haven't been revealed yet, they seem to be internally

accumulating data and
testing

things like, "What style does this person have?"
We've done this test before at Pcode,

so I think they're probably using
the same formula now

. What I mean by community exploration
is, if you think of user profiles

as Midjourney's version of Instagram, I
share, like, follow, and

things like that.
Some people might

wonder, "Is this content interesting enough?"
It wasn't my intention, but

I've been conducting a small experiment in a group chat. The group chat
address is in "More," and it's

a morning scene from the Midjourney Korea group chat
. Someone

shares an SREF seed as a morning greeting
, and someone else who receives that greeting also

shares an SREF seed as a good morning
. They share like this, and someone else

mixes their own
SREF seed with this SREF seed,

and so
on. They share like this

. Someone else
has collected thumbnails using Style Explorer

. They share things like, "This
is the number, and I created this."

In this way, you can share manually on KakaoTalk.
If you create an image in the

Instagram style of the content you just saw
, or find a style in the style finder and

click the post button,
you can think, 'That's shared on my Social Media.' I think,

' Wouldn't this be interesting
enough for Midjourney users

?' Speaking of experimenting with
personalization and draft mode, personalization is currently

being tested on a private server
with guides and moderators, as I mentioned earlier.

They say they're optimizing the speed and responsiveness of Draft mode, but I have absolutely no idea what they're talking about
. I don't know the specifics, but their goal is to enhance

individual creative control and style
consistency . Pcode, for example,

offers excellent performance for stylistic consistency, but
I think they're looking for ways to further enhance

that. I have absolutely
no idea what they're talking about

regarding individual creative control
. They're optimizing

the speed and responsiveness of Draft mode,
and they say "current performance is below expectations." This

was mentioned last week:
they generate images in under

a second . That made me a little uneasy
. For Midjourney users

, speed isn't that important.
Quality is paramount

. High quality and
speed can be impressive, but

high speed and low quality are both key considerations.
For Midjourney users, trading quality for speed

is simply unacceptable. That's why, when
Draft mode first came out, the developers

boasted, "We love it because it's so
fast!" only to receive complaints

from Midjourney users, "Why is the quality so bad?"
What they're talking about here

is that the current performance falls short of expectations.
They've achieved the speed, but

the image quality
is poor. I think Midjourney understands

that this speed will only be meaningful
if it's increased to a level that satisfies

users . If
these two features succeed,

7.1 will be released
. If not, they

'll likely be reflected in 8.
The success of these features seems to be key to the release

of V7.1. They're talking about
a community event, a sailboat meetup. They're saying

they're launching a Midjourney community meetup in San Francisco.
Think of it

as a lightning-fast
meetup , held around

sunset on a large catamaran,
and can accommodate 50 to 90 people at a time.

It's a very large ship. The Midjourney team and the engineering
team will participate in

the meeting, asking and answering
development-related questions . Participants can share feedback and

interact with creators.
Once user profiles are complete,

they'll follow each other back and forth.
This seems to be the vision

the Midjourney CEO has in mind . Around sunset
, I pulled up a mat and looked for catamarans

. They had two of them, and
they were called catamarans. The middle one

had a spacious deck.
I imagined

they'd charter a large boat like this, with about 100 people
chatting about Midjourney, blah blah blah.

I was wondering
, "Why on earth would they even think of

that?" Then I looked at a map and
realized San Francisco was right there

. San Francisco
is surrounded by water on three sides, a bit like Busan

. There are a lot of IT startups
in this area,

and while working in San Francisco
, I used to go to the beach every weekend and saw someone's yacht party.

I thought, " If we're successful, we should do something like that,
" and I think that might happen

this time . They said, "If it's popular,
we plan to expand the Midjourney boat global tour event

." What I'm curious
about is whether they'll come to Korea for that global event

. One of the Midjourney developers
is Korean , and I'm wondering if they

can promote Korea well enough to bring it to Korea
. Here's some news about Midjourney TV.

A new feature is that
when you click

3, 4, or 5, content
is displayed in a grid format in real time

. However, it doesn't seem to have been updated yet.
For now, it's just the same.

I mentioned that large grids provide a more immersive experience, using the "Architect" scene from the movie "The Matrix
." I think the "Architect" scene

is referring to
this scene, and I

think the idea of
​​displaying content on a large wall like this

is what makes it more immersive
. The Matrix is ​​a classic now,

and watching it gives you a sense of the experience of the Midjourney
development team

. The 5-grid system
is heavy, but it's visually

striking. We're preparing various views based on these diverse opinions,
but the problem is that most people probably don't know what Midjourney TV is

. The Midjourney TV
address is Midjourney.tv.

If you go there, it
randomly plays Midjourney videos

. It makes a nice backdrop
for a large wall at an event

, but I don't think many people are aware of this
. Midjourney is

constantly adding features to new content as it becomes available
. People aren't really interested

, but I'm still paying attention
, so if you go

to Midjourney.tv for fun
, you can check out various Midjourney videos

. It's about community participation and rankings,
and they say

they're holding
a style ranking party

and encouraging user participation, but I have no idea where they're getting it from.
Regarding this style ranking

party, if you look here, there's a way I made with shorts
to get one hour of Fast Hour in five minutes.

Someone in the group chat
confirmed that

there were cases where you could get an hour by doing just 10 a day.
For those who don't have enough Fast Hours, it's very easy

to get Fast Hours,
so if you safely do 50 to 100,

you can earn an hour a day
. I think I'm the one who encourages user participation

more, but anyway,
I'm encouraging it this way, and the collected

feedback data is reflected in the direction of future aesthetic and model
improvements. We

This means that if you work hard on something, it
will be used to improve

the performance of Midjourney, which we use
. If you want to

improve the service I use,
I'll give you an hour of your time for free, so please participate in this style ranking

. Merchandising & Swag I heard
that official Midjourney goods

are being prepared to launch, called Swag. What will
it be? Instead of manufacturing them in-house, they will

outsource OEM production in the form of an official vendor partnership,
and the goal is not to make them profitably

, but to create fun items
centered around the community . Goods.

Fun, community-centered items, not for
profit. The moment I saw this, I thought, "Did

the person who planned this
see merchandise for K-pop idols ?" I

'm half excited and half worried about what kind of items will come out, but my anticipation is that
it will be something that fits Midjourney perfectly, something

we couldn't have imagined .
My worry is that if it's something like a keychain or

a doll,
I'll be in trouble. Since

Midjourney is mostly
staffed by programmers,

it's hard to expect any brilliant ideas
. However, since this

isn't a direct development project but an official vendor partnership,
I think if we choose the right vendor,

something innovative might emerge
. Regarding company growth and leadership,

a new executive team
has joined to improve operational efficiency and resolve bottlenecks.

I think they've brought in someone as a director. They're planning to hire additional engineers and project
management staff, so

with the new head,
they're hiring the relevant personnel

. The founder
is currently preparing an official announcement video and managing new projects

. First, the founder is David.
The guy who announced these office hours

suddenly mentioned the founder, so I was like,
"What's going on?" He then said it was an official announcement video,

so I thought, "What's going on?" But
the related details are coming up later. They're

discussing undisclosed projects and future plans
. There are numerous undisclosed projects,

including functional projects, community
programs, and

hardware-related projects
. They're constantly discussing

functional projects . For
personalized community programs

, we're developing a Midjourney version of
Socal Media. I don't know the hardware, but

it's somewhere between VR and AR .
We're awaiting internal approval and media

preparation.
This is the video the founder mentioned on the previous page is preparing

. It's supposed to be a hardware announcement,
so it looks like something will be coming out soon.

We recently
did a "World's Big East Problem"

list, asking, "Why are we even doing this?
" The list is complete and

will soon be voted on by the community,
but I have no idea where

it ends
. It seems

like they're working on a bigger picture
. I shared some information about

yesterday's Office Hours, and
what I'm

particularly interested in, as you can see in the thumbnail,
is Style Finder. I'm looking forward to seeing

how Style Finder can take the existing text
prompting method to the next level

. I just happened to share information about Style Finder today, and
as if it were pre-planned,

my presentation tomorrow on YouTube Live will
be about "Arriving at the AI ​​Style Singularity:

The Beginning of the Prompt 2.0 Era
. " So, I was planning to cover

exactly this in tomorrow's livestream:
how the Style Finder

works, how we should use it, and
what we need to prepare

. I happened to hear about
this topic during Office Hours

yesterday, so
I was like, "Thank you!" Tomorrow's

livestream isn't just about me
; I 'll also be covering

topics related to Yoon Seok-jang's AI music,
and Kim Han-jae's Midjourney and

how to create an AIToon using Nano Banana .
I took a peek at the presentation materials,

and they 're incredibly detailed.
Of course, I 've prepared quite diligently to keep up

with them. Tomorrow's
livestream is at 4 PM. Since this is a

celebration of reaching 4,000 subscribers
, I hope you'll tune in,

leave comments, and
join us in the chat.

Please tune in again,
and don't be late, it's 4 PM. Today's key

word is "limit."
Looking at Midjourney's progress so far,

it's clear that we're constantly
pushing and overcoming our limitations . As we

use Midjourney,
we're evolving along the way, and the pace of development is so

fast that we sometimes don't realize
we've surpassed our limits

. We usually indicate
that we've surpassed our limits

and gone up a level by marking it as a version. This is
the topic I'll cover in tomorrow's live, and

it was briefly touched upon during today's Office Hour.
It's about

Prompt 2.0, which transcends the limits of prompts
. So, I decided to use the term "limit" in the hopes that,

along with Midjourney,
we, as users, can also develop and overcome these limits

. With the word "limit" as our starting point,
we'll end this session here
. Thank you.
